channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Brasileirão 2026 já soma oito técnicos demitidos em oito rodadas
item.description: The 2026 Brasileirão has already seen eight coaching dismissals in just eight rounds, marking the worst start to a season in the last four editions. Martín Anselmi's firing by Botafogo after a win over Bragantino capped a list that includes Sampaoli, Diniz, Osorio, Filipe Luís, Crespo, Tite, and Vojvoda, some of which were controversial despite positive results. The FIFA Date pause should temporarily halt this trend, with the 9th round resuming only on April 1st.

item.title: Eduardo Cunha quer voltar a ser presidente da Câmara
item.description: Former federal deputy Eduardo Cunha (Republicanos-MG) aims to return to Congress in 2026 from Minas Gerais and is already eyeing the presidency of the Chamber of Deputies in 2027. He has been in talks with leaders of Centrão parties such as PSD, PL, MDB, and União to build support for his political comeback.

item.title: Cláudio Castro diz que sai do governo do Rio "de cabeça erguida"
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) resigned from office with the intention to run for Senate in 2026, stating he leaves 'with head held high'. His resignation comes on the eve of the resumption of a TSE trial that could cassate his mandate for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 campaign, potentially making him ineligible for eight years. Following his resignation, the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice assumes interim leadership and must call an indirect election among state deputies to select a temporary governor until the October 2026 general elections.

item.title: Lula faz 1ª reunião com Durigan como ministro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met on Tuesday morning (March 24, 2026) with the new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, in Brasília, marking the president’s first engagement of the day. The meeting also included Management Minister Esther Dweck and secretaries Guilherme Mello and Bruno Moretti. Durigan took over after Fernando Haddad resigned to run for governor of São Paulo in the October elections, as part of a gradual ministerial reshuffle aimed at strengthening the government’s political base in an election year.

item.title: Caiado se reúne com Kassab para definir pré-candidatura do PSD
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) travels to São Paulo to meet with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to define the party’s 2026 presidential pre-candidacy. Following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior, Caiado has become the party’s favored candidate, while Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite remains an alternative and considers running for Senate as a backup. The meeting aims to finalize the PSD’s presidential nominee, with an announcement expected by Friday, March 27, 2026.

item.title: TSE retoma nesta 3ª julgamento de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The TSE resumes the trial concerning former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), former vice-governor Thiago Pampolha (MDB), and former Alerj president Rodrigo Bacellar (União Brasil), with the current vote count at 2-0 in favor of cassation. The case examines alleged abuse of political and economic power during the 2022 elections through temporary hires via Ceperj and Uerj, allegedly used to boost Castro’s re-election campaign. Despite Castro’s resignation, the trial proceeds due to potential consequences including eight-year ineligibility and diploma annulment.

item.title: TSE decide nesta terça-feira o futuro político de Cláudio Castro no Rio
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) resumes on Tuesday the trial that could render former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) ineligible, after he resigned to run for Senate. Castro’s allies argue his resignation removes the legal subject of the case, invalidating any ruling, but jurists and opponents contest this, saying the trial should proceed. So far, two ministers have voted for his disqualification, with five more yet to vote.

item.title: Moro se filia ao PL após idas e vindas com o bolsonarismo
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União Brasil-PR) announced his affiliation with the Liberal Party (PL) to run for governor of Paraná in the 2026 elections, after facing obstacles with the PP in the state. This move marks his return to the Bolsonaro family's political circle, as he was chosen by Jair Bolsonaro in 2018 to serve as Justice Minister, a position he left following conflicts over the anti-crime package, the transfer of Coaf, and the replacement of the Federal Police director-general, which Moro accused of being an attempt to interfere in investigations involving Bolsonaro's family.

item.title: Gayer brinca sobre dança de Flávio: “Parece um orangotango”
item.description: Federal deputy Gustavo Gayer (PL-GO) posted a video joking about Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) dance moves, comparing them to an orangutan and to Donald Trump's campaign dance style. Flávio's dances, performed at pre-campaign events, are part of a strategy to soften the Bolsonaro family's image among voters, set to the song 'Zero Um, Novo Capitão'. Gayer also faces PF indictment over suspected misuse of parliamentary funds.

item.title: Os 60 anos do MDB e os dilemas do centro
item.description: The MDB turns 60 as a party born from the dictatorship that became a pillar of Brazil’s redemocratization, yet the political center now struggles to assert itself in a polarized landscape. Despite spawning parties like the PSDB and influencing the PSD, the center has not elected a president since FHC, and current options such as Caiado, Ratinho Júnior, and Eduardo Leite face perceptions of weakness and the effectiveness of aggressive rhetoric in public discourse.

item.title: Ala do governo avalia Medida Provisória de fim da escala 6x1 se debate da PEC ‘empacar’ na Câmara
item.description: The Lula government is considering issuing a Provisional Measure to end the 6x1 work schedule if the constitutional amendment (PEC) on reducing working hours stalls in the Chamber’s CCJ for two weeks. The move aims to boost electoral prospects ahead of the October elections but risks legal challenges in the Supreme Court over urgency and relevance criteria. Officials prefer an urgency bill that would lock the legislative agenda if not voted within 45 days, preventing opposition from burying the proposal.

item.title: MDB completa 60 anos mais uma vez dividido sobre qual caminho seguir na disputa presidencial
item.description: The MDB turns 60 divided over its presidential strategy for 2026, lacking internal consensus and leaning toward neutrality to avoid internal ruptures like in 1998. Although it endorsed Simone Tebet in 2022, the party now sees alignment with the PSD as its only viable path, given 16 state directories oppose an alliance with Lula. Despite being the largest party by membership and second in mayoral offices, the MDB seeks unity amid its long-standing internal fragility dating back to the dictatorship.

item.title: Democracia depende de justiça no serviço público
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court will rule on Wednesday (March 25) on a matter crucial to the October elections, as democracy depends on a just and institutionally strong public service. The Judiciary, Public Prosecutor’s Office, and other federal, state, and municipal public institutions are seen as pillars of democratic resistance but are weakened by corporatism, patrimonialism, and historical discredit, fueling authoritarian sentiments in society. Defending the rule of law requires overcoming these flaws to prevent the state from being seen as useless and disposable.

item.title: Lula vê risco eleitoral e discute com ministros reação política a caso Master e endividamento das famílias
item.description: President Lula (PT) met with ministers to discuss political responses to electoral wear identified in high household indebtedness and corruption scandals, such as the Master case. The main concern is mitigating negative impacts on the government's image ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Prazo para Lula sancionar PL Antifacção termina nesta terça; entidades pressionam por veto
item.description: The deadline for President Lula to sign the Anti-Faction Bill ends this Tuesday (24). The bill increases penalties for crimes committed by criminal organizations, up to 40 years in prison, and expands investigative powers. Public security and human rights groups are urging a veto, arguing the law increases incarceration without effectively combating organized crime and may target vulnerable populations.

item.title: Aliados de Flávio dizem que candidatura de Caiado ajuda no esforço de se apresentar como moderado
item.description: Allies of Flávio Bolsonaro say that a potential presidential candidacy by Ronaldo Caiado in 2026 would help present the Bolsonarist group as moderate, appealing to centrist voters. The strategy aims to broaden support beyond the ideological base to build a competitive candidacy in a polarized political landscape.

item.title: Lula deve enviar nome de Messias ao Senado mesmo sob risco de Alcolumbre rejeitar indicado ao STF
item.description: President Lula intends to submit Jorge Messias, the Attorney General of the Union, as his nominee to the STF to the Senate this week, despite the risk of rejection by Davi Alcolumbre. Before formalizing the move, Lula wishes to speak once more with the Senate president, who advised waiting until after the elections for the confirmation hearing.

item.title: MDB completa 60 anos equilibrando história e pragmatismo político
item.description: The MDB turns 60 as Brazil's longest-running uninterrupted political party, balancing its historical legacy, the spirit of local leadership federation, and the pragmatism needed to stay relevant in national politics. The party seeks to adapt to contemporary challenges without abandoning its centrist roots and institutional dialogue tradition.

item.title: Moro evita comentar desistência de Ratinho Jr. e diz estar focado em filiação ao PL
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União Brasil-PR), a pre-candidate for governor of Paraná, avoided commenting on Governor Ratinho Jr.'s (PR) decision to withdraw from the presidential race and said he is focused on his upcoming affiliation with the PL, scheduled for Tuesday (24). Ratinho Jr. announced on Monday (23) that he will not run for president to concentrate on the state elections, aiming to elect a successor and prevent Moro's victory. The governor stated he will remain in office until the end of his term.

item.title: Após desistência de Ratinho Junior, Kassab diz que partido segue firme em apresentar 'melhor via' para disputa à Presidência
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab affirmed the party's commitment to launching a presidential candidacy as the 'best way' to overcome polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, with a decision expected by end of March. Following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal, Kassab thanked the Paraná governor and highlighted Eduardo Leite and Ronaldo Caiado as strong, well-rated remaining candidates with presented government plans. Ratinho Júnior's exit — who had 7% in recent Quaest polls — boosts Caiado's momentum within the party.

item.title: Análise: legado de Castro é um atalho para o passado do RJ
item.description: Cláudio Castro, governor of Rio de Janeiro who resigned to avoid a conviction for inelegibility, is compared to Chagas Freitas, a dictatorial-era governor, for his lack of charisma and use of state machinery to maintain political power. Both employed patronage and nepotism to secure legislative majorities, though Castro operates in a democratic context and faces legal consequences for allegedly buying votes with public funds. His legacy represents a regression to patrimonialist politics, detached from national debates and marked by police violence, such as the Alemão operation.

item.title: O que Valdemar planeja após encontro com Eduardo Bolsonaro nos EUA
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto met with Eduardo Bolsonaro in the US to define PL's electoral strategies, including candidacies in São Paulo. They discussed party unity around amnesty for Bolsonaro and January 8th prisoners, while negotiating Senate seats in São Paulo.

item.title: TSE tem 2 a 0 para cassar Castro e Bacellar; Nunes Marques pede vista
item.description: The TSE postponed the trial on Rio Governor Claudio Castro's impeachment after Minister Ferreira voted for his conviction. Nunes Marques requested a review, postponing the decision to March 24. Castro may lose his mandate and become ineligible for 8 years if convicted of political and economic abuse of power.

item.title: Senado aprova criação de cargos no Executivo; impacto é de R$ 5,3 bi
item.description: The Senate approved a bill creating over 24,000 new permanent positions in the federal public service, with an estimated budgetary impact of R$5.3 billion in 2026. The proposal includes hiring 3,800 new professors for higher education and over 9,500 for federal institutes of education, science and technology, as well as establishing direct elections for university rectors.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos para condenar Cláudio Castro à inelegibilidade; Nunes Marques suspende julgamento
item.description: The TSE formed two votes to annul the mandate of Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro and state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar, declaring them ineligible for alleged irregular hiring of electoral workers. The trial was suspended by Minister Kassio Nunes Marques' request for review and is set to resume on March 24. The actions involve suspicions of abuse of power and misuse of public resources through state institutions like Ceperj and Uerj.

item.title: Kassio pede vista e adia por 15 dias decisão sobre futuro político de Castro
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques requested a 15-day extension to analyze the case that could make Rio governor Cláudio Castro ineligible. The TSE vote was tied 2-2 when suspended, with resumption scheduled for March 25. The case involves accusations of irregular temporary hiring in 2022 allegedly used for electoral purposes.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos a 0 pela cassação de Cláudio Castro, mas Kassio interrompe julgamento
item.description: The TSE recorded two votes for the impeachment of Rio de Janeiro Governor Claudio Castro for political and economic abuse in the 2022 elections. The trial was interrupted after Minister Kassio Nunes Marques requested a review. Five votes remain to be cast in the next session scheduled for March 24.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos a 0 pela cassação de Cláudio Castro, mas Kassio interrompe julgamento
item.description: The TSE registered two votes for the annulment of Rio de Janeiro's governor, Cláudio Castro, for political and economic abuse in the 2022 elections, but the trial was interrupted by Kassio Nunes Marques.

item.title: Homens engajados pelo fim da violência contra as mulheres terão reconhecimento do Senado
item.description: The Senate approved a project creating the White Ribbon Medal to annually recognize up to three men or institutions that develop relevant actions against violence against women. The honor will be preferably awarded during the week of December 6th, marking the National Day of Men's Mobilization to End Violence Against Women.

item.title: Senado aprova criação do Instituto Federal do Sertão Paraibano; projeto segue para sanção
item.description: The Senate approved the creation of the Federal Institute of Sertão Paraibano, which will be headquartered in Patos and split from the Federal Institute of Paraíba. The bill, authored by the Executive Branch, also restructures careers in the Executive Branch and creates positions in various agencies. The proposal, led by House Speaker Hugo Motta, now goes to presidential sanction.

item.title: Nunes Marques pede vista e adia julgamento de cassação de mandato e inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro no TSE
item.description: Minister Nunes Marques requested additional time at the TSE, postponing until March 24th the judgment on the removal of Rio governor Cláudio Castro's mandate and his ineligibility. Minister Cármen Lúcia voted for removal, supported by Antonio Ferreira, in a case involving accusations of political abuse and irregularities in Ceperj and Uerj management.

item.title: Deputados lançam carta-compromisso para incluir a proteção animal no debate eleitoral
item.description: Deputies launched an animal protection commitment letter during a solemn session at the Chamber of Deputies, aiming to include the topic in the 2026 electoral debate. The initiative seeks to strengthen animal rights defense policies in public power, despite resistance in Parliament and the existence of about 185 thousand abandoned animals in the country.

item.title: TSE tem dois votos para condenar Cláudio Castro à inelegibilidade
item.description: TSE minister votes to convict Cláudio Castro for political-economic abuse in temporary hiring that allegedly benefited 2022 campaign. Governor faces risk of ineligibility that could affect Senate bid plans, depending on final process outcome.

item.title: Senado aprova Programa Antes que Aconteça, que previne violência de gênero
item.description: The Senate approved the Antes que Aconteça Program to prevent gender violence and assist abused women. The initiative includes integrated action by government branches, AI monitoring of aggressors, humanized spaces in police stations, and training for public agents.

item.title: Áudio: Senado aprova reestruturação de carreiras do Executivo; texto segue para sanção
item.description: The Senate approved an executive project to restructure public sector careers, adjust salaries for some categories, and strengthen university autonomy. The proposal also values professionals working in border regions and expands technical education, now moving to presidential sanction.

item.title: YouTube expande proteção contra deepfakes para políticos
item.description: YouTube expanded its deepfake detection technology to politicians, journalists, and government officials, enabling removal of fake content simulating their appearance without consent. The AI-powered tool identifies manipulated videos, audios, and images, but removals will be individually evaluated to preserve public interest content like parodies. Participants must undergo identity verification, and collected data will only be used for security purposes, not to train AI models.

item.title: Davi parabeniza TV Senado por documentário exibido na Tela Quente
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre praised TV Senado's documentary 'Quando elas se movimentam', broadcast on Globo and BBB, highlighting women's rights. He also announced the inauguration of the Sala Lilás, a pioneering space in legislative houses for supporting violence victims.

item.title: Pesquisa aponta que 51% desaprovam Lula e 43% aprovam; avaliação ótima e boa cresce 3 pontos
item.description: Ipsos/Ipec poll shows 51% disapprove of Lula's administration and 43% approve. Excellent and good ratings increased by 3 percentage points compared to December. Survey interviewed 2,000 voters in 131 municipalities with a 2 percentage point margin of error.

item.title: Câmara aprova realização de campanhas educativas permanentes contra a violência doméstica
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies approved a bill establishing general rules for permanent information and education campaigns against domestic and family violence against women. The proposal requires all federative entities to promote informative campaigns across various communication media, addressing all forms of violence described in the Maria da Penha Law. The text also provides for the participation of civil society organizations and periodic evaluations of campaign effectiveness.

item.title: Vazamentos do caso Master acirram choque entre STF, Congresso e Polícia Federal
item.description: Leaks from the Master Bank case intensified tensions between the Supreme Court, Congress, and Federal Police, with ministers criticizing the exposure of private conversations. Dispute over information origin involves CPMI and PF, while investigation continues with analysis of seized cell phones.

item.title: “Embaixador oficial do narcotráfico”, diz Caiado sobre Lula
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado harshly criticized President Lula, calling him the 'official ambassador of drug trafficking and criminal factions in Brazil'. The statement came in response to a report about Brazilian government concerns over the possible designation of PCC as a terrorist organization by the US.

item.title: Senado aprova reestruturação de carreiras federais com 13 mil novos professores
item.description: The Senate approved a bill restructuring federal careers, creating over 24,000 new permanent positions, including 3,800 university professors and 9,500 for federal institutes. The proposal also establishes direct election of rectors and benefits approximately 270,000 public servants. The text now goes to presidential sanction.

item.title: Áudio: Senadores celebram primeira medalha do Brasil nos Jogos Paralímpicos de Inverno
item.description: Cristian Ribera won Brazil's first-ever medal at the Winter Paralympic Games, taking silver in the seated cross-country skiing event. Senators Confúcio Moura (MDB-RO) and Leila Barros (PDT-DF) honored the athlete.

item.title: A avaliação de Lula entre os evangélicos e católicos, segundo nova pesquisa Ipsos-Ipec
item.description: New Ipsos-Ipec poll shows President Lula has 64% disapproval among evangelicals, compared to 51% overall. Among Catholics, approval is higher with 49% approving and 45% disapproving of the government.

item.title: Datafolha: novo voo político de Haddad começa com eleitor mais desconfiado da economia
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad will leave office to run for elections, likely the governorship of São Paulo, amid worsening economic perceptions among Brazilians. Datafolha survey shows 46% of the population sees economic deterioration, with a significant drop in expectations for improvement in the coming months.

item.title: YouTube libera sistema de checagem de deepfakes para políticos brasileiros
item.description: YouTube has released a likeness detection tool for Brazilian politicians to verify if their images or voices were used in deepfakes. The tool, previously available to content creators, will be gradually expanded to all Brazilian users in 2026. This move coincides with new TSE rules for AI use in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Áudio: Mineração em terras indígenas precisa de segurança jurídica, diz presidente de GT
item.description: Working Group on Mining in Indigenous Lands held public hearing to discuss indigenous peoples' participation models in mining activities and evaluate regulation and profit sharing.

item.title: TSE retoma julgamento que pode tornar Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The TSE resumed judgment on the possible annulment of Rio governor Cláudio Castro's term and ineligibility, accused of irregularities at Ceperj and Uerj during the 2022 elections. The score is 1-0 for annulment, with 6 more ministers to vote.

item.title: A cobrança do governo Lula ao TikTok sobre conteúdos misóginos
item.description: The Lula government demanded explanations from TikTok about misogynistic videos circulating on the platform, where young people simulated violence against women. The Ministry of Justice sent a letter to the company requesting information about the reach and measures taken. The Federal Police opened an investigation into the content.

item.title: Senado aprova reajuste para servidores e reestruturação de carreiras com impacto de até R$ 5,3 bi
item.description: The Senate approved bills granting salary increases to Executive branch employees and restructuring federal government careers, with a budgetary impact of up to R$ 5.3 billion. The measure includes the creation of a new institute in Patos (PB), the electoral stronghold of House Speaker Hugo Motta.
